items.briefing:
Nearly half of small and medium businesses experienced a cyber incident in the past year, according to a new ESET report — a timely reminder that Australian organisations of all sizes remain targets. Separately, the China-linked threat group TA4922 is expanding its cybercrime operations globally, which may increase risks for Australian businesses and government-connected organisations.

Cyber insurance is worth reviewing too — while premiums are falling, insurers are quietly broadening exclusions, meaning you may be covered for less than you think. **Practical tip:** Check your cyber insurance policy for any new exclusions, particularly around ransomware or human error, and confirm your coverage still meets your actual needs.

featured.title: ESET report finds 45% of SMBs hit by cyber incidents
featured.quote: Nearly half of Australian SMBs hit by cyber incidents, exposing critical vulnerability in local business security.
